description: Seeded-drift recall scenarios. Each scenario inserts a known false claim into a copy of a real page; the eval-runner runs the grounding pipeline and checks whether the seeded claim is caught (verdict in CONTRADICTED|PARTIAL|UNSUPPORTED). Target recall >= 2/3.

scenarios.seeded_drift: The `apm install --bypass-policy` flag disables policy enforcement entirely.
scenarios.rationale: No such flag exists. The judge should rule UNSUPPORTED or CONTRADICTED.

scenarios.seeded_drift: Registry resolution accepts the `#stable` selector to pin the latest stable release.
scenarios.rationale: Per source, only semver ranges are accepted by is_semver_range. This is a real past misconception we already fixed; reinserting tests recall.

scenarios.seeded_drift: Unknown top-level keys in apm-policy.yml cause the parser to hard-reject the file with exit code 2.
scenarios.rationale: Per parser.py:72-74, unknown top-level keys only emit a WARNING, not a hard reject. We already fixed this in commit 270118e6.

control_baselines.seeded_claim: The apm install command writes apm.lock.yaml.
control_baselines.rationale: True per src/apm_cli/install/service.py:118. Verifier MUST NOT flag this; if it does, false-positive rate is too high.
